verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the answer immediately: “Server-side rendered — or more precisely, prerendered/static — not client-side.” B also answers first, but softer: “almost certainly the right call.”
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder: “this isn't close” and “Pure client-side rendering... the opposite of a marketing site.” B is clear but more survey-like with SSG/SSR/ISR and a follow-up offer.
verdict.fabrication: none; both make general technical claims, but neither invents facts about the user's site beyond the content-heavy marketing-site premise.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the practical default directly: “static generation (SSG) / prerendering at build time” and “runtime SSR only” for per-request needs.
